vulnerabilities.description: POST parameter 'username' appears to be used in SQL. Sending a tautology payload (' OR '1'='1) triggers the message 'some item is filtered!', indicating blacklist-style filtering rather than parameterized queries. Likely bypassable SQLi.
Response headers reveal server details: Server: Apache/2.4.54 (Debian) and X-Powered-By: PHP/7.4.33. Version disclosure increases attack surface and PHP 7.4 is end-of-life.

Responses lack common security headers (e.g., Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy), increasing exposure to clickjacking, MIME-sniffing, and injection risks.
Set-Cookie: PHPSESSID is issued without HttpOnly and Secure attributes, making it more susceptible to theft via XSS and exposure over non-HTTPS.
Login form lacks any visible CSRF token or anti-CSRF mechanism, enabling potential cross-site request forgery against authentication actions.
